# Vendored font sources

## `font8x8_*.h` — 8x8 monochrome bitmap fonts

Author: Daniel Hepper `<daniel@hepper.net>`
Upstream: https://github.com/dhepper/font8x8
License: **Public Domain**

Quote from the header of each file:

> 8x8 monochrome bitmap fonts for rendering
> Author: Daniel Hepper <daniel@hepper.net>
> License: Public Domain
>
> Based on:
>     Marcel Sondaar
>     International Business Machines (public domain VGA fonts)
> License:
>     Public Domain

These files are vendored verbatim from
`https://github.com/dhepper/font8x8` (commit HEAD as of the import
date) so a source diff stays tractable. The conversion to Rust const
data lives under `src/fonts/*.rs` and is generated by
`tools/gen_fonts.py` (a plain text parser — one-shot, output
committed).

## Bit layout

Each glyph is 8 bytes, one byte per row. Within a byte, **bit 0 is
the leftmost pixel** (LSB-first). The `BitmapFont::bit_order` field
carries `BitOrder::Lsb` for these and preserves the convention
downstream into the upload + draw paths.

## Why these instead of e.g. Linux `font_8x8.c`

Linux's kernel fbcon fonts are GPL-2.0 licensed even though the
bitmap data itself is universally considered PD (IBM released the
original ROM font in 1981 with no copyright claim). Shipping under
a plain PD grant via a project dedicated to PD redistribution is
simpler to reason about for this GPL-licensed repo. The visual output
is the same 8x8 IBM-VGA look.
